---
title: "STAGES_plotting"
author: "Seán F"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r libs, include=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)
library(circular)
library(corrplot)
library(lmtest) 
library(purrr)
library(grid)
library(xfun)
library(purrr)
library(effectsize)
library(broom)
library(tidyr)
library(ggeffects)
library(car)
library(broom)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(patchwork)
library(broom)
library(readr)
})
```
#load ata
```{r Data load}
#This code assumes that the other two .Rmd's have been ran first, without this you will not be able to access most of the files needed in the resultant chunks
#this is the demographics file available from the NSRR site entitled stages-dataset-0.3.0.csv
raw_demographics <- read.csv("your_file_path_here")
#All below files are from the Actigraphy_workflow.Rmd
#This is the participants lowest 12 hours of activity file 
l5 <- read.csv("your_file_path_here")
#This is each participatns CPD score file
cpd <- readr::read_csv("your_file_path_here")
#This is each participatns SJL  file
sjl <- readr::read_csv("your_file_path_here")
#These are all same location, just need to load the vals separately
std_dev_midsleep <- readr::read_csv("your_file_path_here")
std_dev_duration <- readr::read_csv("your_file_path_here")
std_dev_onset <- read.csv("your_file_path_here")
std_dev_offset <- read.csv("your_file_path_here") 
#SRI score
sri <- readr::read_csv("your_file_path_here")
#IS score
is <- readr::read_csv("your_file_path_here")
#need this for the mean calculations to be completed in the demographics section
sleep_timing <- read.csv("your_file_path_here")
target_subject_ids <- unique(psg_results_table$Subject_ID)
#Clean and filter data by subject_id, some columns have different headings so jut standardize
sri <- sri %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Subject_ID = stringr::str_replace(File, "_binary\\.csv$", "")) %>%
  dplyr::rename(subject_id = Subject_ID) %>%
  dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
is <- is %>%
  dplyr::rename(subject_id = Subject_ID) %>%
  dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
#Be sure values are taken from appropraite subjects
sjl <- sjl %>% dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
std_dev_midsleep <- std_dev_midsleep %>% dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
std_dev_onset <- std_dev_onset %>% dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
std_dev_offset <- std_dev_offset %>% dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
std_dev_duration <- std_dev_duration %>% dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
cpd <- cpd %>% dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% target_subject_ids)
#Clean metric datasets, just removes fluff
cpd <- tibble::as_tibble(cpd) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, CPD = mean_cpd)
sjl <- tibble::as_tibble(sjl) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, SJL)
std_dev_midsleep <- tibble::as_tibble(std_dev_midsleep) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, SD_Midsleep = midsleep_sd_hours)
std_dev_duration <- tibble::as_tibble(std_dev_duration) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, SD_Duration = duration_sd_hours)
std_dev_onset <- tibble::as_tibble(std_dev_onset) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, SD_Onset = onset_sd_hours)
std_dev_offset <- tibble::as_tibble(std_dev_offset) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, SD_Offset = offset_sd_hours)
sri <- tibble::as_tibble(sri) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, SRI)
is <- tibble::as_tibble(is) %>% dplyr::select(subject_id, IS)
#Safe join helper (prevents .x/.y)
safe_left_join <- function(x, y, by) {
  dup <- intersect(names(x), names(y))
  dup <- setdiff(dup, by)
  if (length(dup) > 0) {
    x <- dplyr::select(x, -dplyr::any_of(dup))
  }
  dplyr::left_join(x, y, by = by)
}
#Merge all metrics
cor_df_complete <- purrr::reduce(
  list(cpd, sjl, std_dev_midsleep, std_dev_duration, sri, is, std_dev_onset, std_dev_offset),
  ~safe_left_join(.x, .y, by = "subject_id")
) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

#Define demo variables of interest, more/less can be added for covariates later 
demo_vars <- c("age", "sex", "gad_score", "phq9_score", "rmeq_score")
#Build analysis_df
analysis_df <- psg_results_table %>%
  dplyr::rename(subject_id = Subject_ID) %>%
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(demo_vars)) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(cor_df_complete, by = "subject_id") %>%
  dplyr::distinct(subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    raw_demographics %>% 
      dplyr::transmute(
        subject_id  = subject_code,
        age         = modified_dem_0110,
        sex         = factor(dem_0500, levels = c("F", "M")),
        gad_score   = gad_0800,
        phq9_score  = phq_1000,
        rmeq_score  = cir_0700
      ),
    by = "subject_id"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(age >= 18) #can remove/change if needed 
#Can't use 4 psg stages due to multicollinearity
baseline_vars <- c(
  "SRI","SJL","IS",
  "N2_percent","N3_percent","REM_percent","REM_Lat_min","SOL"
)

analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(stats::complete.cases(dplyr::select(., dplyr::all_of(baseline_vars)))) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

#Final IDs used everywhere downstream
included_ids <- unique(analysis_df$subject_id)
#check ===
cat("Baseline N:", nrow(analysis_df), "\n")
cat("Distinct subjects:", dplyr::n_distinct(analysis_df$subject_id), "\n") # just in case there are some duplicates
```
#Mean times and MEQ 
```{r Descriptives for table}
#need to compute circular measn for time vals
circular_mean_hours <- function(mins_or_hours) {
  vals_min <- ifelse(mean(mins_or_hours, na.rm = TRUE) < 24, mins_or_hours * 60, mins_or_hours)
  radians <- circular((vals_min %% 1440) / 1440 * 2 * pi, units = "radians")
  mean_rad <- mean.circular(radians)
  mean_hour <- as.numeric(mean_rad) * (1440 / (2 * pi)) %% 1440
  return(mean_hour / 60)
}
included_ids <- unique(analysis_df$subject_id)
sleep_timing_cleaned <- sleep_timing %>%
  dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% included_ids) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    onset_dt  = as.POSIXct(onset, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tz = "UTC"),
    offset_dt = as.POSIXct(offset, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tz = "UTC"),
    onset_hr  = as.numeric(format(onset_dt, "%H")) +
                as.numeric(format(onset_dt, "%M")) / 60 +
                as.numeric(format(onset_dt, "%S")) / 3600,
    offset_hr = as.numeric(format(offset_dt, "%H")) +
                as.numeric(format(offset_dt, "%M")) / 60 +
                as.numeric(format(offset_dt, "%S")) / 3600
  )

#Per-subject means FIRST
per_subject_means <- sleep_timing_cleaned %>%
  dplyr::group_by(subject_id) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_onset_hr   = circular_mean_hours(onset_hr),
    mean_offset_hr  = circular_mean_hours(offset_hr),
    mean_TST        = mean(TST_mins, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_SPT        = mean(SPT_mins, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_WASO       = mean(WASO_mins, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_SEI_pct    = mean(SEI_pct, na.rm = TRUE),   #<-- ADDED
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#Group-level means
timing_summary <- per_subject_means %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    onset_mean_hr     = mean(mean_onset_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    onset_sd_hr       = sd(mean_onset_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    offset_mean_hr    = mean(mean_offset_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    offset_sd_hr      = sd(mean_offset_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    SPT_mean_min      = mean(mean_SPT, na.rm = TRUE),
    SPT_sd_min        = sd(mean_SPT, na.rm = TRUE),
    TST_mean_min      = mean(mean_TST, na.rm = TRUE),
    TST_sd_min        = sd(mean_TST, na.rm = TRUE),
    WASO_mean_min     = mean(mean_WASO, na.rm = TRUE),
    WASO_sd_min       = sd(mean_WASO, na.rm = TRUE),
    SEI_mean_pct      = mean(mean_SEI_pct, na.rm = TRUE), 
    SEI_sd_pct        = sd(mean_SEI_pct, na.rm = TRUE)     
  )
overall_summary_table <- tibble::tibble(
  Variable = c(
    "Sleep Onset",
    "Sleep Offset",
    "Sleep Period Time (min)",
    "Total Sleep Time (min)",
    "WASO (min)",
    "Sleep Efficiency (%)"
  ),
  Mean     = round(c(
    timing_summary$onset_mean_hr,
    timing_summary$offset_mean_hr,
    timing_summary$SPT_mean_min,
    timing_summary$TST_mean_min,
    timing_summary$WASO_mean_min,
    timing_summary$SEI_mean_pct
  ), 2),
  SD       = round(c(
    timing_summary$onset_sd_hr,
    timing_summary$offset_sd_hr,
    timing_summary$SPT_sd_min,
    timing_summary$TST_sd_min,
    timing_summary$WASO_sd_min,
    timing_summary$SEI_sd_pct
  ), 2),
  N        = rep(nrow(per_subject_means), 6),
  `Mean (HH:MM)` = c(
    v_decimal_to_hm(timing_summary$onset_mean_hr),
    v_decimal_to_hm(timing_summary$offset_mean_hr),
    sprintf("%02d:%02d", floor(timing_summary$SPT_mean_min / 60), round(timing_summary$SPT_mean_min %% 60)),
    sprintf("%02d:%02d", floor(timing_summary$TST_mean_min / 60), round(timing_summary$TST_mean_min %% 60)),
    sprintf("%02d:%02d", floor(timing_summary$WASO_mean_min / 60), round(timing_summary$WASO_mean_min %% 60)))
)
#MEQ categories taken from NSRR website
meq_by_subject <- raw_demographics %>%
  dplyr::distinct(subject_code, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::filter(subject_code %in% included_ids) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    MEQ_category = dplyr::case_when(
      cir_0700 >= 4  & cir_0700 <= 7  ~ "Definitely Evening Type",
      cir_0700 >= 8  & cir_0700 <= 11 ~ "Moderately Evening Type",
      cir_0700 >= 12 & cir_0700 <= 17 ~ "Neither Type",
      cir_0700 >= 18 & cir_0700 <= 21 ~ "Moderately Morning Type",
      cir_0700 >= 22 & cir_0700 <= 25 ~ "Definitely Morning Type",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    MEQ_category = factor(
      MEQ_category,
      levels = c(
        "Definitely Evening Type",
        "Moderately Evening Type",
        "Neither Type",
        "Moderately Morning Type",
        "Definitely Morning Type"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(subject_id = subject_code, MEQ_score = cir_0700, MEQ_category) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(MEQ_category, subject_id)

meq_tally <- meq_by_subject %>%
  dplyr::count(MEQ_category, name = "n") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(MEQ_category)

print(overall_summary_table)
print(meq_tally)
#Save eveything to .csv if easier
write.csv(overall_summary_table, "your_path_here", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(meq_tally, "your_path_here", row.names = FALSE)
```
#Demographics
```{r Demograhics}
#LOad demographics file and tally 
raw_demographics <- read.csv("path_same_as_earlier_demographics_file")
demographics <- raw_demographics %>%
  filter(subject_code %in% included_ids) %>%
  distinct(subject_code, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%  #One row per subject
  transmute(
    subject_id = subject_code,
    sex = na_if(trimws(dem_0500), ""),
    sex = na_if(sex, " "),
    age = modified_dem_0110,
    BMI = dem_0800,
    race = dem_1000,
    race = ifelse(race == "", NA, race),
    smoker = current_cigarette_smoker,
    exercise = soclhx_0501,
    gad_score = gad_0800,
    phq9_score = phq_1000,
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c("F", "M"), labels = c("Female", "Male")),
    race = factor(race, levels = 1:6, labels = c(
      "White/Caucasian",
      "Black or African American",
      "American Indian or Alaska Native",
      "Asian",
      "Pacific Islander",
      "Two races or some other race"
    )),
    smoker = factor(smoker, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    exercise = factor(exercise, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
  )

#Initialize results table
demographics_table <- tibble(
  Variable = character(),
  N = integer(),
  `% or Mean (SD)` = character()
)
#Helper for continuous vars: exclude missing from N
add_continuous <- function(var, label) {
  vals <- demographics[[var]]
  n_non_missing <- sum(!is.na(vals))
  m <- mean(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
  s <- sd(vals, na.rm = TRUE)
  demographics_table <<- add_row(demographics_table,
    Variable = label,
    N = n_non_missing,
    `% or Mean (SD)` = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", m, s)
  )
}
add_categorical <- function(var, label) {
  vals <- demographics[[var]]
  vals <- vals[!is.na(vals)]  #exclude NA from counts
  tab <- table(vals)
  n <- sum(tab)
  
  #Add main category header
  demographics_table <<- add_row(demographics_table,
    Variable = label,
    N = n,
    `% or Mean (SD)` = ""
  )
  
  #Add each outcome as a separate row
  for (outcome in names(tab)) {
    count <- tab[outcome]
    percent <- sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * count / n)
    demographics_table <<- add_row(demographics_table,
      Variable = paste0("  ", outcome),  #Indent subcategories
      N = as.integer(count),  #Show individual count in N column
      `% or Mean (SD)` = paste0("(", percent, ")")  #Just percentage in parentheses
    )
  }
}
#Add sample size at the top just to see how many responses are in each section as it's likely to vary
sample_size <- length(included_ids)
sample_size_row <- tibble(
  Variable = "Sample size",
  N = sample_size,
  `% or Mean (SD)` = ""
)
demographics_table <- bind_rows(sample_size_row, demographics_table)
#Build the table
add_continuous("age", "Age (years)")
add_categorical("sex", "Sex")
add_continuous("BMI", "BMI")
add_categorical("race", "Race")
add_categorical("smoker", "Current Smoker")
add_categorical("exercise", "Exercise Frequently")
add_continuous("gad_score", "GAD-7 Score")
add_continuous("phq9_score", "PHQ-9 Score")
print_demographics_table <- function(tbl) {
  bold_vars <- c(
    "Sample size", "Age (years)", "Sex", "BMI", "Race",
    "Current Smoker", "Exercise Frequency", "GAD-7 Score", "PHQ-9 Score"
  )
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(tbl))) {
    is_bold <- tbl$Variable[i] %in% bold_vars
    heading <- if (is_bold) paste0("\033[1m", tbl$Variable[i], "\033[0m") else tbl$Variable[i]
    cat(heading, "\n")
    cat("N:", tbl$N[i], "\n")
    cat(tbl$`% or Mean (SD)`[i], "\n\n")
  }
}
print_demographics_table(demographics_table)
write.csv(demographics_table,"your_path_here")
```
#Mean STV scores
```{r STV means}
#These were for a separate table designed to delineate sleep-related characteristics of population
sleep_timing_vars <- c(
   "SRI",
  "SJL",
  "IS",
  "CPD",
  "SD_Midsleep",
  "SD_Onset",
  "SD_Offset",
  "SD_Duration"
)
sleep_timing_descriptives <- analysis_df %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(sleep_timing_vars)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = dplyr::everything(),
    names_to = "Measure",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Measure) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(Value)),
    mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    range = paste0(
      round(min(Value, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      "–",
      round(max(Value, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  sleep_timing_descriptives,
  "your_path_here",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

#Mean PSG scores
```{r PSG scores}
#same as prev chunk,not mandatory to run but may be beneficial to see characterisitcs of analysis sample
PSG_vals <- c(
   "TST_min", 
   "SEI", 
   "WASO_min",
   "N1_percent", 
   "N2_percent",
   "N3_percent",
   "REM_percent",
   "REM_Lat_min"
)

PSG_descriptives <- analysis_df %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(PSG_vals)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = dplyr::everything(),
    names_to = "Measure",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Measure) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(Value)),
    mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    range = paste0(
      round(min(Value, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      "–",
      round(max(Value, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  PSG_descriptives,
  "your_path_heere",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```
#Account for no. of people from each site
```{r Study site}
#Load actigraphy data and extract locality prefix
analysis_df_names <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(site_code = substr(subject_id, 1, 4))
#Map site codes to locations
site_lookup <- tibble::tibble(
  site_code = c("BOGN", "GSBB", "GSDV", "GSLH", "GSSA", "GSSW", "MSNF", "MAYO", "MSQW", "MSTR", "STLK", "STNF", "MSMI", "MSTH"),
  location = c("South Carolina", "Pennsylvania", "Pennsylvania", "Pennsylvania", "Pennsylvania", "Pennsylvania",
               "Niagara Falls", "Minnesota", "Toronto", "Ontario", "Missouri", "California", "Ontario", "Toronto")
)
#Join and count
location_summary <- analysis_df_names %>%
  distinct(subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  left_join(site_lookup, by = "site_code") %>%
  count(location, name = "n_subjects") %>%
  mutate(
    percent = round(n_subjects / sum(n_subjects) * 100, 1),
    formatted = paste0(location, "\t", n_subjects, " (", percent, "%)")
  )

print(location_summary %>% select(location, formatted))
write.csv(location_summary, "your_path_here")
```
#Correlation coefficient of all stvs
```{r Correlations}
sleep_metrics_df <- analysis_df %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(sleep_timing_vars)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), as.numeric))

corr_table <- combn(sleep_timing_vars, 2, simplify = FALSE) %>%
  purrr::map_dfr(function(v) {
    v1 <- v[1]
    v2 <- v[2]
    tmp <- sleep_metrics_df %>%
      dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(c(v1, v2))) %>%
      tidyr::drop_na()
    ct <- suppressWarnings(
      stats::cor.test(
        tmp[[v1]], tmp[[v2]],
        method = "spearman", exact = FALSE
      )
    )
    tibble::tibble(
      Comparison = paste(v1, "vs", v2),
      r = round(unname(ct$estimate), 2),
      p_raw = ct$p.value  # keep raw numeric for correction
    )
  }) %>%
  #Benjamin Hochberg method to correct for multiple comparisons
  dplyr::mutate(
    p_adj = p.adjust(p_raw, method = "BH"),
    `p-value` = ifelse(p_raw < 0.001, "<0.001", formatC(p_raw, format = "f", digits = 3)),
    `p-value (BH)` = ifelse(p_adj < 0.001, "<0.001", formatC(p_adj, format = "f", digits = 3))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Comparison, r, `p-value (BH)`)

write.csv(
  corr_table,
  "your_file_path",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```
#correlation plots
```{r corr plots primary stv's}
primary_metrics <- c("SJL", "SRI", "IS") # can change these depending on what associations you are looking to examine
sleep_metrics_df <- analysis_df %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(primary_metrics)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), as.numeric)) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()
#Function to create individual scatter plot with Spearman correlation
create_scatter_plot <- function(data, x_var, y_var, panel_label, plot_title) {
  #Calculate Spearman correlation on accoutn of non-normality of data
  cor_test <- cor.test(data[[x_var]], data[[y_var]],
                       method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  r_val <- round(cor_test$estimate, 2)
  p_val <- cor_test$p.value
  #Format r-value with significance stars
  if (p_val < 0.001) {
    r_text <- paste0("r=", sprintf("%.2f", r_val), "***")
  } else if (p_val < 0.01) {
    r_text <- paste0("r=", sprintf("%.2f", r_val), "**")
  } else if (p_val < 0.05) {
    r_text <- paste0("r=", sprintf("%.2f", r_val), "*")
  } else {
    r_text <- paste0("r=", sprintf("%.2f", r_val))
  }
  #Full variable names
  x_label <- switch(x_var,
                    "SJL" = "Social Jetlag (hours)",
                    "SRI" = "Sleep Regularity Index",
                    "IS"  = "Interdaily Stability")

  y_label <- switch(y_var,
                    "SJL" = "Social Jetlag (hours)",
                    "SRI" = "Sleep Regularity Index",
                    "IS"  = "Interdaily Stability")
  base_size <- 16     
  title_size <- 18
  axis_title_size <- 18
  axis_text_size <- 17
  panel_label_size <- 20
  r_text_size <- 16
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = .data[[y_var]])) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2.6, color = "black") +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue", linewidth = 1.2) +
    labs(
      x = x_label,
      y = y_label,
      title = plot_title
    ) +
    annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
             label = r_text,
             hjust = 1.05, vjust = 1.2, size = r_text_size/ggplot2::.pt,
             fontface = "bold") +
    annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf,
             label = panel_label,
             hjust = -0.3, vjust = 1.2, size = panel_label_size/ggplot2::.pt,
             fontface = "bold") +
    theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = title_size, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = axis_title_size),
      axis.text  = element_text(size = axis_text_size),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 14, 10, 10)
    )

  return(p)
}

#Create the three plots with titles
p1 <- create_scatter_plot(sleep_metrics_df, "SJL", "SRI", "A",
                          "Social Jetlag vs Sleep Regularity Index")
p2 <- create_scatter_plot(sleep_metrics_df, "SJL", "IS", "B",
                          "Social Jetlag vs Interdaily Stability")
p3 <- create_scatter_plot(sleep_metrics_df, "SRI", "IS", "C",
                          "Sleep Regularity Index vs Interdaily Stability")

#Combine plots
combined_plot <- patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 3)

#Display
print(combined_plot)

#Save (bigger height helps readability a lot for 3 panels)
ggsave(
  "your_file_path",
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 18,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)
```
#%Models' for all DV's
```{r Primary models }
dvs <- c("SRI","SJL","IS") #Can reorient these depending on what associations you are looking to investigate
raw_predictors  <- "N2_percent + N3_percent + REM_percent + REM_Lat_min + SOL"
demo_predictors <- paste(raw_predictors, "+ age + sex")
full_predictors <- paste(demo_predictors, "+ phq9_score + gad_score + rmeq_score")

model_list <- list(
  "Raw PSG only"           = raw_predictors,
  "Add demographics"       = demo_predictors,
  "Add PHQ9, GAD7 & rMEQ"  = full_predictors
)

#Fit models and store results
model_results <- list()
vif_results   <- list()

for (dv in dvs) {
  for (label in names(model_list)) {

    fmla  <- as.formula(paste(dv, "~", model_list[[label]]))
    model <- lm(fmla, data = analysis_df)
    n_used <- nobs(model)

#Model-level fit stats
    fit_df <- broom::glance(model) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        outcome    = dv,
        model_type = label,
        n_obs      = n_used,
        R2         = r.squared,
        adj_R2     = adj.r.squared
      )

#Standardized betas (post-hoc; no CI)
    std_df <- effectsize::standardize_parameters(model) %>%
      dplyr::rename(term = Parameter) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        term,
        Std_Beta = Std_Coefficient
      )

    #Raw coefficients + CI
    tidy_df <- broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
      dplyr::left_join(std_df, by = "term") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(outcome = dv, model_type = label, n_obs = n_used) %>%
      dplyr::left_join(fit_df, by = c("outcome", "model_type", "n_obs")) %>%
      dplyr::select(
        outcome, model_type, n_obs, R2, adj_R2,
        term,
        Std_Beta,            #β
        estimate,            #B
        std.error,           #SE
        conf.low, conf.high, #95% CI (B)
        statistic, p.value
      )

#VIFs
    predictor_terms <- attr(terms(model), "term.labels")
    vif_raw <- tryCatch(
      car::vif(model),
      error = function(e) setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(predictor_terms)), predictor_terms)
    )

    vif_df <- tibble::tibble(
      outcome    = dv,
      model_type = label,
      term       = predictor_terms,
      VIF        = as.numeric(vif_raw[predictor_terms]),
      n_obs      = n_used
    )

    model_results[[paste(dv, label, sep = "_")]] <- tidy_df
    vif_results[[paste(dv, label, sep = "_")]]   <- vif_df
  }
}

#Combine outputs
all_model_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(model_results)
all_vif_results   <- dplyr::bind_rows(vif_results)

#View
cat("\nModel Estimates\n")
print(all_model_results)

cat("\nVIF Results\n")
print(all_vif_results)

#Save
write.csv(
  all_model_results,
"your_path_here", row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  all_vif_results,
  "your_path_here",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```
#% Secondary model 
```{r Secondary model}
dvs <- c("SD_Midsleep","SD_Duration","SD_Onset", "SD_Offset","CPD") #these are just the remaining DV's
raw_predictors  <- "N2_percent + N3_percent + REM_percent + REM_Lat_min + SOL"
demo_predictors <- paste(raw_predictors, "+ age + sex")
full_predictors <- paste(demo_predictors, "+ phq9_score + gad_score + rmeq_score")
#Exact same covariates as prev model
model_list <- list(
  "Raw PSG only"           = raw_predictors,
  "Add demographics"       = demo_predictors,
  "Add PHQ9, GAD7 & rMEQ"  = full_predictors
)
model_results <- list()
vif_results   <- list()

for (dv in dvs) {
  for (label in names(model_list)) {

    fmla  <- as.formula(paste(dv, "~", model_list[[label]]))
    model <- lm(fmla, data = analysis_df)
    n_used <- nobs(model)

#Model-level fit stats
    fit_df <- broom::glance(model) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        outcome    = dv,
        model_type = label,
        n_obs      = n_used,
        R2         = r.squared,
        adj_R2     = adj.r.squared
      )

#Standardized betas (post-hoc; no CI)
    std_df <- effectsize::standardize_parameters(model) %>%
      dplyr::rename(term = Parameter) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        term,
        Std_Beta = Std_Coefficient
      )

#Raw coefficients + CI (B scale)
    tidy_df <- broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
      dplyr::left_join(std_df, by = "term") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(outcome = dv, model_type = label, n_obs = n_used) %>%
      dplyr::left_join(fit_df, by = c("outcome", "model_type", "n_obs")) %>%
      dplyr::select(
        outcome, model_type, n_obs, R2, adj_R2,
        term,
        Std_Beta,            #β
        estimate,            #B
        std.error,           #SE
        conf.low, conf.high, #95% CI (B)
        statistic, p.value
      )

#VIFs
    predictor_terms <- attr(terms(model), "term.labels")
    vif_raw <- tryCatch(
      car::vif(model),
      error = function(e) setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(predictor_terms)), predictor_terms)
    )

    vif_df <- tibble::tibble(
      outcome    = dv,
      model_type = label,
      term       = predictor_terms,
      VIF        = as.numeric(vif_raw[predictor_terms]),
      n_obs      = n_used
    )

    model_results[[paste(dv, label, sep = "_")]] <- tidy_df
    vif_results[[paste(dv, label, sep = "_")]]   <- vif_df
  }
}

#Combine outputs
all_model_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(model_results)
all_vif_results   <- dplyr::bind_rows(vif_results)

#View
cat("\n Model Estimates\n")
print(all_model_results)

cat("\nVIF Results\n")
print(all_vif_results)

#Save
write.csv(
  all_model_results,
  "your_path_here",
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  all_vif_results,
  "your_path_here",
  row.names = FALSE
)
vars_needed <- c("SRI", "N2_percent", "N3_percent", "REM_percent", "REM_Lat_min", "SOL",
                 "age", "sex", "phq9_score", "gad_score", "rmeq_score")
sum(stats::complete.cases(analysis_df[, vars_needed]))
```
